title: aristida funiculata var. mallica
intro: Aristida funiculata var. mallica, also known as Mallick grass, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Mallick grass has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating skin infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Mallick grass are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which are applied topically to treat wounds and skin conditions.
